title: Explain the Future interface in Java. How is it used?

answers.junior: Future represents the result of an asynchronous computation. You can check if it's done and get the result.
answers.middle: Future lets you submit tasks to ExecutorService and retrieve results later. Use get() to block until done, isDone() to check status.
answers.senior: Future enables asynchronous programming. Use get(), cancel(), isDone(), isCancelled(). Combine with Callable for result retrieval. Handle InterruptedException and ExecutionException.
answers.tricky: Use Future for async task management. Prefer CompletableFuture for advanced workflows. Analyze blocking, cancellation, and exception handling. Monitor thread pool health and task completion.
